title: Daily Wrap — 21 May 2026
body: 4 meetings — Kilmore, Penrith, Redcliffe, and Tamworth — made up the day’s 36-race harness action.

On the clock, HAPPY TOO YA set the mark with a 1:55.0 mile rate over 1720 metres at Penrith. For pure dominance, GREY CADILLAC's 23-metre win at Penrith was the one. It was not the only big-margin result either, with MCCRIKEY NZ (11m at Tamworth) also winning decisively.

The training story had a few threads. At Redcliffe, J J Hutchinson prepared 3 winners and G C Hewitt prepared a winning pair; at Penrith, K A Morris finished with two winners; at Tamworth, A J Missen, L M Osborn, and M A Formosa all finished with two winners.

In the sulky, a few names kept coming up. At Kilmore, James Herbertson produced a 3-win meeting; at Redcliffe, Brendan Barnes drove 3 winners and Paige Bevan finished with two winners; at Penrith, Robert Morris finished with two winners; at Tamworth, Cooper Griffiths, Michael Formosa, and Tom Ison all struck twice.
The roughest winner was DESIGNER JEWEL, landing at $51 at Redcliffe. Favourite backers had a few bruises as well. Tamworth saw NIFTY STUDLEIGH ($1.70) and ARNIES ARMY NZ ($1.85) beaten.
Leaders had mixed fortunes across the meetings. Front-runners had the better of it at Penrith (5 of 7); while leaders found it harder at Redcliffe (2 of 10).

One of the standout performances in defeat came from THE GOLDEN HOOK, who produced the day’s fastest calculated finishing burst — an estimated individual last half of 56.1 — despite finishing 3rd at Kilmore.
